crisis.label: Elevated
crisis.detail: Jun 22-23: roadmap agreed Switzerland; IAEA access disputed; mine-clearing Day 7; tail 8%

topAlert: JUN 23 ROADMAP: US-Iran Switzerland talks conclude; IAEA inspectors invited (Vance); Iran disputes; Goldman cuts Q4 Brent to $80; Brent $75.04 Jun 25; tail 8%
commodityScenario: Jun 23: Roadmap agreed Switzerland; IAEA access progress contested. Brent $75.04 (TradingEconomics Jun 25, +1.8%; -24% from Jun 3 $98.57 peak). Goldman Sachs cuts Q4 Brent to $80 from $90 — expects GCC exports at pre-war levels by end-Jul. Mine-clearing Day 7 of 30-day MOU mandate; physical Hormuz reopening on central route: late-2026. Ras Laffan LNG restart not before late Aug 2026. Tail 8%. Commodity markets continuing to price supply resumption — LNG and fertilizer supply lag limits pace of normalisation.
marketContext: Roadmap agreed Jun 22-23 Switzerland; Brent $75.04 (TradingEconomics Jun 25, +1.76%); Goldman Q4 $80; mine-clearing Day 7 of 30-day mandate; tail 8%.

todaysEvents.summary: US-Iran Phase 2 talks: roadmap agreed Jun 22-23 Switzerland; IAEA inspectors 'invited' per Vance; Iran FM disputes any new access agreement (Al Jazeera/CNBC/Times of Israel)
todaysEvents.delta: Down: First round of Phase 2 nuclear talks concluded early Jun 23 at Burgenstock. Vance: 'roadmap toward final deal within 60 days agreed; Iranians agreed to invite IAEA inspectors this week - major milestone.' Lebanon deconfliction mechanism agreed. Iran FM disputes: no IAEA meeting held; no new access planned beyond existing NPT/safeguards. IAEA director Grossi says inspections 'will indeed take place' and discussions on dates/procedures beginning. Technical talks continuing in Switzerland throughout the week. Tail 8%.

todaysEvents.summary: Brent $75.04 (TradingEconomics Jun 25 confirmed, +1.76%); partial rebound after Jun 24 -5.3% crash on tanker traffic surge; Goldman cuts Q4 Brent to $80 from $90 (Investing.com)
todaysEvents.delta: Down: Brent $75.04 (TradingEconomics Jun 25 confirmed, +1.76% from $73.87 Jun 24 close). Jun 24 was the big crash day: -5.3% as tankers transited Hormuz and Goldman Sachs cut Q4 Brent forecast to $80 from $90 - expects Persian Gulf crude exports normalised to pre-war levels by end-Jul, a month ahead of prior estimate. WTI ~$69.95 est. (below $70 on Jun 25, CNBC). Gold $4,040.30 (TradingEconomics Jun 25, +1.02%). iTraxx Asia IG ~128bp est. (-4bp); ASEAN HY ~460bp est. (-7bp). EM FX continuing to strengthen: USD/PHP ~55.20 est., USD/IDR ~15,700 est.

todaysEvents.summary: Hormuz mine-clearing: Day 7 of 30-day MOU mandate (Jun 19 start); tanker traffic recovering (20+ transits Jun 19, highest since Jun 2); Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026 (Jun 26)
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Mine-clearing Day 7 of 30-day MOU mandate from Jun 19 signing. Conventional minesweeping/underwater drones may require 40-50 days before P&I clubs re-enter; Pentagon internal estimate: up to 6 months for full clearance. Tanker transits recovering: 20+ transits Jun 19 (highest since Jun 2 vs 100+ pre-war); still well below normal. Central deep-water channel remains closed pending clearance of ~80 mines. Ras Laffan LNG restart not before late Aug 2026 regardless of mine-clearing pace. Nuclear sequencing: Iran deputy FM - nuclear talks begin only after frozen funds released.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Roadmap agreed Jun 22-23 Switzerland; IAEA inspectors invited per Vance; Lebanon deconfliction mechanism agreed. Ceasefire holds; Iran dilutes uranium under IAEA; US sanctions lifted progressively; Iran oil sales resume. Hormuz mine-clearing proceeds (~30 days per MOU mandate, up to 6 months for full clearance); P&I clubs re-enter progressively; Ras Laffan restart late Aug 2026. Brent normalises $70-85/bbl on supply resumption (Goldman Q4 $80 forecast). Phase 2 nuclear deal in 60-day window. Probability raised to 67% on roadmap progress.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Implementation delays. Nuclear sequencing dispute (Iran withholds IAEA cooperation until frozen funds released; Iran FM disputes IAEA access claim) stalls mine-clearing beyond MOU mandate; Hormuz physically inaccessible 60-90 days past signing. Ras Laffan slips to Q4 2026. Brent stays $80-95/bbl as market discounts partial supply resumption. iTraxx Asia IG settles 130-150bp; JKM holds $15-18/MMBtu. IRGC hardliner resistance to terms or US congressional opposition to sanctions relief are the main stress vectors.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Deal collapse. Iran hardliners or IRGC reject MOU implementation; nuclear sequencing terms break down; US Congress blocks sanctions relief; Israel unilateral strikes restart hostilities. Hormuz re-closed; Brent spikes $110-130/bbl; mine-clearing timeline extends 12+ months. Probability lowered to 8% from 10% (D109) on roadmap agreement Jun 22-23. Key triggers: frozen-assets impasse causes Iran to withdraw; IDF unilateral strike; US congressional veto on sanctions relief.

volatilityDay.note: Thu - Brent ~$75.04 carried (Jun 25 confirmed; Jun 26 not yet confirmed). Roadmap agreed Jun 22-23; IAEA access disputed but Grossi says inspections 'will take place.' Mine-clearing Day 7 of 30-day mandate. Goldman Q4 $80 Brent. iTraxx ~128bp est. EM FX strengthening. Tail 8%.

priceNarratives.Brent: Pre-shock ~$65 → $126 wartime high intraday (Day 59, Apr 30, CNBC/CNN) → $98.57 (Jun 3 D93 peak; Qeshm strike + Kuwait airport + Rubio rejection) → $75.04 (Jun 25, TradingEconomics confirmed, +1.76%; -24% from D93 peak). Roadmap agreed Jun 22-23 Switzerland; Goldman cuts Q4 to $80 from $90 (Investing.com) — GCC exports normalised by end-Jul. Mine-clearing Day 7 of 30-day mandate from Jun 19. Working range $70-85 base; $80-95 stress; $110-130 on deal collapse (tail 8%).
priceNarratives.JKM LNG: Baseline $9.5 → $23.40/MMBtu (Day 20, Reuters/Platts) → $18.86/MMBtu (last confirmed Jun 12, TradingEconomics; carried). Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026 even on deal; mine-clearing 30-day mandate from Jun 19 (physical reopening late-2026 on central route); JKM supply lag means LNG stays elevated well after roadmap agreement.
priceNarratives.TTF Gas: Pre-shock ~$34/MWh → €49.97/MWh (Day 33) → €49.50/MWh (Jun 3 D93 peak) → €41.54/MWh est. (Jun 26; carried; roadmap progress continued de-escalation unwinding crisis premium)
priceNarratives.Credit: iTraxx Asia IG est. ~128bp (Jun 26, -67bp from D93 195bp peak; -11bp from D109 139bp); ASEAN HY est. ~460bp (-120bp from D93 580bp; -28bp from D109 488bp); trim CDS protection; remove fully at confirmed mine clearance + physical Hormuz reopening (~115-120bp target); tail 8% (deal collapse spikes to 200-230bp)
